name: Stained Glass Candy
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 cups Lily white syrup (or Karo, must be colorless)
10 cups Icing sugar (can be used over and over)
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix the white sugar, white syrup, and water in a medium-sized pot.
2. Add the food coloring you want (green for spearmint, orange for orange).
3. Bring to a boil, and continue boiling until the candy thermometer reaches 300°F degrees. This can take up to 20 minutes.
4. Stir to mix all the ingredients, but once it starts to boil, do not touch. The pot must be big enough to allow for doubling as it rises while boiling.
5. While the candy mixture is boiling, shape a large piece of tin foil by folding up the sides and ends (about 3 feet long). Cover the foil completely with icing sugar and bank up the sides with icing sugar.
6. When the candy mixture has reached 300°F, remove from heat. Let sit about 10 seconds and stir to cool slightly.
7. Add about 1 to 1-1/2 teaspoons of oil-based flavorings depending on how strong you want the flavor. Stir until mixed (may bubble a bit).
8. Pour the mixture onto the icing sugar and use a rubber spatula to scrape all the mixture out of the pot. Place the pot, spatula, and candy thermometer in a sinkful of hot soapy water.
9. As the candy is cooling, keep checking by trying to pull up the edges. Once it reaches the consistency that you can lift it slightly, start cutting quickly.
10. Cut off a chunk and then cut into bite-sized pieces. The outside will cool first, so watch it carefully. If it hardens too quickly, it will shatter like glass.
11. Continue working around until all is cut. Shake off excess icing sugar over a bowl and allow to cool thoroughly.
12. Pack in airtight containers (Zip-Loc Freezer bags work well).
13. This recipe makes about 2 lbs of candy. Once you have completed all the desired flavors, set up the bags with about 10 pieces each and seal them. These make great Christmas gifts for family members, mail carriers, garbage collectors, paperboys, school teachers, and your kids' friends.
